Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A tacheometer is set up 60 m from a vertically held staff. The stadia readings taken with a horizontal sight are 1.350 m and 1.920 m. If the focal length of the object glass is 20 cm and the distance between the object glass and vertical axis of the tachometer is 10 cm, then the stadia interval is:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A traffic survey for a proposed two lane single carriageway in plain terrain recorded an average daily traffic of 900 vehicles per day three years before the planned completion of construction. It was found that 70% of total traffic comprises commercial vehicles. The road is designed for 15 years design life starting from the year of completion. The annual traffic growth rate is 7% and the vehicle damage factor for plain terrain may be taken as 3.5. Assume a lane distribution factor
of 0.75 for a two lane single carriageway. The cumulative number of standard axles (in million standard axles) that should be considered for the pavement design is :

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In a cement concrete pavement, the maximum width of the expansion joint is 2.5 cm and the maximum rise in temperature is 30°C. The maximum length of concrete slab (in meters) that can be laid continuously without causing excessive thermal stress is _____ .
(Take α = 10 × 10–6 /°C; Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 41.67
Solution

41.67 (40.5 - 42.5)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A moving car observer collected travel time data by running North-South on a stretch of urban road of length 3.5 km. Based on the observations, determine the average journey speed (in kmph) for the stretch for vehicles moving from South to North direction. (Answer upto two decimal places)

 
NAT
Correct answer: 28.29
Solution

28.29 (27.50 - 29.50)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The contour interval on a map is 15 m. If the upward gradient of 1 in 20 is required to be drawn between two points, then the horizontal equivalent will be:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A thin bituminous surface pavement is constructed in a heavy rainfall area. For a design speed of 60 kmph, the radius beyond which no superelevation is required is _____ m. (rounded off to nearest integer).

NAT
Correct answer: 640
Solution

640 (640 - 640)

A calibrated speed density model is given by

 
where values of C and m are 54.6 km/hr and 220 veh/km respectively. The correct option(s) indicating the parameters of the above speed-density relation is/are:
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, d)

In a stadia survey, the staff is held vertical and the instrument is fitted with an anallactic lens.
Upto what vertical angle can a sloping distance be assumed equal to the horizontal distance if the permissible error is not to exceed 1 in 400?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33

An airport site has an elevation of 350 m and the airport reference temperature is 31.8°C. The percentage temperature correction on the runway will be:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A bituminous mix specimen has a bulk specific gravity of 2.40 and a theoretical maximum specific gravity of 2.61. The bitumen binder constitutes 5.4% by weight of the total mix and has a specific gravity of 1.10. Based on this information, identify which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A vane is sighted at a point 2800 m away from an instrument station. The vane is fixed at 2.5 m above the foot of the staff. The observed angle of elevation is 2°15'. If the reduced level (RL) of the trunnion axis is 150 m, the reduced level of the staff station considering the combined correction for curvature and refraction is _____ m. (Rounded off upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 258.04
Solution

258.04 (257.50 - 258.50)

NAT
Correct answer: 192.12
Solution

192.12 (191.80 - 192.40)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A signalised intersection operates with a total cycle time of 85 seconds. The green time for a particular approach road is 30 seconds, and the corresponding amber time is 4 seconds. The startup loss time is 2 seconds, and the clearance interval is 1.5 seconds. If the saturation headway is 2.6 seconds per vehicle, then the practical capacity for this approach road is :

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A driver with normal vision  can read a 15 cm high letter on a roadside sign from 100 m
away. Another driver travelling with a speed of 72 kmph and having a vision of  can read the same sign only when closer. The driver’s perception-reaction time is 2.5 seconds. The driver (with vision ) applies brakes immediately after seeing the sign. If the deceleration after applying brakes is 3 m/s2, then the distance from the sign at which the vehicle comes to a complete stop is _______ m. (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 50.07
Solution

50.07 (49 - 51)

 
Q18 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is primarily used to distinguish and identify different objects or features on the Earth’s surface in remote sensing?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The spectral signature of an object describes how it reflects or emits electromagnetic radiation across different wavelengths. The unique pattern helps distinguish one object from another in satellite imagery or aerial surveys.

Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A cement concrete rigid pavement 7 m wide (dual lane) is designed with a slab thickness of 300 mm. Longitudinal joints are provided at the centre and at the edges of lane. The coefficient of friction between slab and subgrade is 1.5. Allowable working stress in steel is 125 MPa and bond stress developed between steel and concrete is 1.75 MPa. If the diameter of the tie bars is 12 mm, the maximum centre-to-centre spacing and the length of tie bars required across the longitudinal joint respectively are:
(Assume unit weight of concrete as 24 kN/m3)

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

On a broad gauge railway line, a horizontal curve of radius 1150 m is provided with transition curves at its ends. The cant on this curve was originally set to match an equilibrium speed of 80 km/h. For a newly introduced express train service, the cant deficiency is not to exceed 10 cm.
The maximum permissible speed for the express train service on this curve is :

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q21 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A line was measured on a slope with a tape that was actually 30.05 m long but was recorded as 30 m. The measured slope length was 450 m and the slope angle was 4°. The actual distance (in metres) between the end points of the line is _______ . (Answer upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 449.65
Solution

449.65 (449 - 551)

 
Q22 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A building located close to the inner edge of a two-lane horizontal curve restricts visibility. The curve has a radius of 160 m, and the lateral clearance between the building and the centre line of highway is 9 m. The coefficient of friction is 0.4, the driver’s perception reaction time is 2.5 s and the lane width is 3.75 m. Assuming the curve is long enough and the available sight distance is governed by this obstruction, then the maximum safe speed on this curve is :

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

At a signalised intersection, the arrival rate of vehicles is 24 veh/min and the departure rate during green time is 52 veh/min. The red time and green time are 28 seconds and 32 seconds respectively. Vehicle arrive and depart at a uniform rate. Select the correct statement(s):

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

A two lane horizontal curve of radius 220 m is to be designed in a plain terrain for a design speed of 60 kmph. The curve is located in a region of heavy rainfall and the carriage way width at the curve is 7.62 m. The minimum length of transition curve required is:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

If the reduced level (R.L.) of the starting benchmark is 102.95 m, then the R.L. of the final point where staff is held is ______ (in meters, rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 103.83
Solution

103.83 (103.83 - 103.83)

NAT
Correct answer: 300.5
Solution

300.5 (300.5 - 300.5)

Two instrument stations, A and B, are established an opposite sides of a hill in the same vertical plane and are horizontally 1500 m apart. The RL of points A and B are 360 m and 420 m respectively. The angles of depression from hilltop P to stations A and B are 14° 20′ and 09° 10′
respectively. Neglecting the errors due to curvature of Earth, refraction and collimation, the elevation of hilltop from MSL will be:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q32 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A taxiway is to be designed for safe maneuvering of an aircraft (subsonic) under the following
conditions:
• Wheel base = 17.8 m
• Tread of main landing gear = 6.2 m
• Coefficient of friction between tyre and pavement = 0.15
• Turning speed = 45 kmph
• Width of taxiway pavement = 22.5 m
Based on these conditions, the minimum turning radius (in meters, round off to two decimal places) is _____ .

NAT
Correct answer: 120
Solution

120 (120 - 120)

 
Q33 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A ship is initially 50 km away from a lighthouse of height 40 m above the sea level. A lookout post on the ship is at 10 m above the water surface. If the ship moves at a constant speed of 20 kmph, the time (in minutes) it will take for the lookout post to first sight the lighthouse light will be _____ . (Take radius of earth as 6370 km; Round off to closest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 40
Solution

40 (39 - 41)
